findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image is missing from the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the NC state seal centered at the top; HTML rendering has no seal image

findings.description: The letterhead layout with officials' names on the left and seal/department name centered is not preserved in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF has ROY COOPER/Governor, MICHAEL S. REGAN/Secretary, BRIAN WRENN/Acting Director on the left with the seal and NORTH CAROLINA/Environmental Quality centered. HTML places these as a flat list at the bottom of the page area rather than in their original positions.

findings.description: Underlined text formatting for 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' is preserved but the visual weight differs slightly
findings.evidence: PDF shows underlined text clearly; HTML also shows underlined text but styling is slightly different

findings.description: The footer with DEQ logo, address and phone number is rendered differently in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows the footer at page bottom with DEQ logo inline; HTML shows it as a horizontal line separator with text

findings.description: DEQ logo in footer is missing from HTML
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 footer has the DEQ logo with text; HTML has only text in the footer area

findings.description: Indentation of sub-items under item 4 (water quality parameters) differs between PDF and HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows parameter categories indented under item 4; HTML shows them with less indentation

findings.description: The sub-items under item 5 (A through G) have different indentation in HTML compared to PDF
findings.evidence: PDF shows deeper indentation for A-G items; HTML shows standard list indentation

findings.description: The signature image of Judith A. Wehner is missing from the HTML rendering
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 shows a handwritten signature above the typed name; HTML only shows 'Sincerely,' with no signature image

findings.description: The address block on page 4 has different alignment in HTML vs PDF
findings.evidence: PDF shows the Division address block indented/centered; HTML shows it left-aligned

findings.description: No heading hierarchy exists in the HTML. The document has no h1, h2, or any heading tags. The document title and section structure are not reflected using semantic heading elements.
findings.evidence:
The HTML uses only

,

    ,
  1. , and
    elements. There are no h1-h6 tags anywhere in the document. The RE: subject line, numbered items, and other structural elements lack heading markup.

findings.description: The first image alt text says 'North Carolina state seal' which is reasonable, but the signature image alt text 'Signature of Judith A. Wehner' is accurate and helpful. However, two page-break images have empty alt text.
findings.evidence: Two tags have alt="" which is acceptable for decorative/page-break images.

findings.description: Page headers ('Certified Mail / Mr. Threatt / Page Two/Three/Four') are repeated inline within the HTML content, which is expected from page-level reproduction but creates minor duplication of header/footer content within the flow.
findings.evidence: The page headers appear as div.page-header elements interspersed within the document content, faithfully reproducing the PDF pagination headers.

findings.description: The footer text shows '27699 1612' with a space, whereas the PDF shows '27699-1612' with a hyphen in one instance, and the footer on page 1 shows '27699 1612'. This is a very minor inconsistency in the footer reproduction.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 footer: 'Raleigh, North Carolina 27699 1612'. HTML footer: 'Raleigh, North Carolina 27699 1612'. This matches the PDF footer. On page 4 the PDF shows 'Raleigh, NC 27699-1612' with a hyphen, and the HTML also shows 'Raleigh, NC 27699-1612'. Both are accurate.
